title: Missing lifetime specifier
explanation: This error occurs when the compiler cannot determine the lifetime of a reference in a function signature or type definition. When a function takes multiple references as input and returns a reference, you need to tell the compiler which input lifetime the output is tied to.

fixes.description: Use owned types to avoid lifetimes

tip: Lifetime elision rules handle most cases automatically. You only need explicit lifetimes when there are multiple reference inputs and a reference output.
